decisions.briefing: Markets were mildly positive today (NASDAQ +0.9%, S&P 500 +0.4%) but this is a neutral, not a risk-on, session. Following the lesson from late June, we're holding our 6 positions and keeping the $777 cash reserve rather than forcing new buys into open slots — we'll deploy on a genuine risk-on day (NASDAQ +2% or more) with full multi-signal confirmation.

market_education.summary_en: Both major indices posted modest gains today, with the NASDAQ slightly outperforming the S&P 500, suggesting continued but unenthusiastic buying interest in growth and tech names. There was no single dominant catalyst driving the move — it reads more as steady drift than a decisive risk-on rally. Without a stronger signal, institutional participation appears mixed rather than broadly convicted.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日兩大指數皆錄得溫和升幅，納指升0.9%，標普500升0.4%，反映科技及成長股仍有買盤支持，但力度不算強勁。市場並無明顯單一催化劑帶動，走勢較似溫和上揚多於全面風險偏好回歸。在缺乏更強烈訊號下，機構參與程度顯得參差，未見一致的強烈信心。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: "Dry powder" refers to cash kept on the sidelines, ready to deploy when a strong opportunity appears. On a neutral market day like today, holding cash instead of forcing a purchase preserves flexibility and avoids buying into weak setups — the goal is to have capital ready for genuinely high-conviction moments, not to keep it always fully invested.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「乾火藥」（Dry Powder）指投資者保留在場邊、隨時可動用的現金。在今天這種中性市況下，寧願持有現金而非勉強買入，可以保持靈活性，避免在訊號不強時貿然進場。目標是在真正高把握的機會出現時有資金可用，而不是時刻都要把資金全數投入市場。
